rag_rules: Keep each annex block in the owner scope it was generated for: component-type contract content stays in a component type, while implementation-level bindings stay in a component implementation.
Preserve scope validity during fusion: content that references only interface-level features belongs with that interface owner, and implementation-only references must not be moved into a type scope.
Insert annex content without changing unrelated subcomponents, connections, properties, or other architectural declarations.
generic_rules: Fuse new contract text into an existing valid owner declaration when possible rather than restructuring the model.
Preserve the original AADL architecture and formatting cues as much as possible; make the smallest ownership-aware edit.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped as an AADL annex block using the standard annex agree block form.
Separate type-scope contract material from implementation-scope binding material; do not merge them across owners.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only content into a component type scope.
Do not move or rewrite unrelated architecture elements just to accommodate an annex insertion.
irrelevant_topics: Detailed AGREE expression syntax such as temporal recurrence or nested if-then-else forms
Scalar helper type selection and expression typing diagnostics
Using AADL property names or unit tokens as AGREE variables
